Tests logical AND and OR operations with short-circuit evaluation in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a short script that demonstrates the behavior of logical operators in JavaScript, specifically focusing on short-circuit evaluation. The [`fail`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/and-orfail>) function contains an assertion that will always fail if executed, but due to the short-circuit nature of logical operators, it is never called. The script uses a series of conditional statements with logical `&&` (AND) and `||` (OR) operators to show how JavaScript evaluates expressions. The conditions are structured to ensure that the [`fail`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/and-orfail>) function is not invoked, thus preventing the assertions within it from triggering. This script serves as an educational example to illustrate how logical operators can prevent certain parts of code from executing.
# Functions

### fail<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/and-or.fail}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/and-or.js#L15>)

Triggers an assertion failure and returns true.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Control Flow**:
    - Calls the `assert` function with `0` as the argument, which triggers an assertion failure.
    - Returns `true` after the assertion.
- **Output**: Returns `true`.
